# Filip Kecman - Penetration Tester

> Penetration tester with 5+ years of experience and 200+ security projects completed. Specializing in web application security, mobile app pentesting, API security assessments, cloud security, and secure code review. Based in Belgrade, Serbia, working worldwide.

## About

Filip Kecman is a professional penetration tester and security consultant. He holds the following certifications: CCPenX-AWS (Certified Cloud Pentesting eXpert for AWS, The SecOps Group), CWEE (Certified Web Exploitation Expert, Hack The Box), CWES (Certified Web Exploitation Specialist, Hack The Box), eWPTX (Web Application Penetration Tester eXtreme, INE Security), eMAPT (Mobile Application Penetration Tester, INE Security), and eJPT (Junior Penetration Tester, eLearnSecurity). He is currently pursuing the OSWE (OffSec Web Expert) certification.

Filip is the CEO and Founder of The Free Security (https://thefreesecurity.com), a non-profit organization providing free penetration tests to organizations that need them. He also serves as a Teaching Assistant at the Faculty of Computing in Belgrade.

## Services

- Web Application Penetration Testing
- Mobile Application Penetration Testing (Android & iOS)
- API Security Assessment
- Network Penetration Testing
- Secure Code Review
- Cloud Security Assessment (AWS)
- Social Engineering Testing
- Security Consultation

## Methodologies

Filip follows industry-standard methodologies including OWASP WSTG (Web Security Testing Guide), OWASP Top 10, OWASP API Top 10, OWASP Mobile Top 10, OWASP MASVS (Mobile Application Security Verification Standard), and PTES (Penetration Testing Execution Standard).

## Experience

- Penetration Tester at SecureIT (2025 - Present)
- Teaching Assistant at Faculty of Computing, Belgrade (2024 - 2026)
- Penetration Tester at UN1QUELY (2023 - 2025)
- Freelance Penetration Tester (2021 - Present)

## Contact

- Website: https://kecman.co
- Email: filip@kecman.co
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/filip-kecman/
- The Free Security: https://thefreesecurity.com

## Blog Posts

- [My CCPenX-AWS Review and Experience](https://kecman.co/blog/ccpenx-aws-review-experience.html): A first-hand review of The SecOps Group's Certified Cloud Pentesting eXpert for AWS (CCPenX-AWS) exam. Covers recommended prerequisites (5+ years pentesting, 12+ months cloud), the publicly listed topic areas (enumeration across DNS, S3, EC2, VPC, IAM, CloudFront, EBS, EKS, Route 53, exploitation and lateral movement, security hardening), the 7.5-hour CTF format delivered over VPN, pass threshold over 60% (merit over 75%), one free retake included, AI tools prohibited during the exam, and practical mindset advice on avoiding tunnel vision and maintaining a credentials inventory. Base price £400, available at £100 with the 75-OFF discount code.
- [Supply Chain Attacks in Modern Software Development: The Axios Incident](https://kecman.co/blog/axios-supply-chain-attack.html): A detailed breakdown of the Axios npm package compromise (83 million weekly downloads). Covers what happened technically, why AI coding tools that auto-install dependencies made the attack surface worse, how to verify if a machine was affected, and what developers and security teams can do to protect against supply chain attacks.
- [Why Sensitive Data Should Never Be in a URL](https://kecman.co/blog/sensitive-data-in-urls.html): How tokens in query strings leak through the Referer header, third-party JavaScript (tawk.to, analytics, chat widgets), server logs, CDN logs, browser history sync, and network proxies. Covers Referrer-Policy browser defaults (strict-origin-when-cross-origin in Chrome 85+/Firefox 87+), element-level referrerpolicy overrides, OWASP guidance, and correct mitigations: POST bodies, Authorization headers, short-lived single-use tokens.
- [How I Use AI to Learn Anything (And Why Nothing Is Hard Anymore)](https://kecman.co/blog/how-i-use-ai-to-learn-anything.html): A personal perspective on using AI as a learning tool. How curiosity, open-mindedness, and tools like NotebookLM make it possible to learn anything from penetration testing to SEO. Why the formula for learning has always been the same and AI just removed the friction.
- [PHP Type Juggling: How a Loose Comparison Can Bypass Your Authentication](https://kecman.co/blog/php-type-juggling-authentication-bypass.html): How PHP's loose comparison operator (==) enables authentication bypass through type juggling. Covers direct bypass via JSON integer injection, magic hashes, strcmp exploitation, and chaining MAC forgery into command injection. Includes prevention with strict comparisons and hash_equals().
- [Vulnerability Scan vs. Penetration Test: Why the Cheaper Option Could Cost You More](https://kecman.co/blog/vulnerability-scan-vs-penetration-test.html): Why a vulnerability scan and a penetration test are fundamentally different services. Why pentests cost what they cost, what you're risking when you give someone access to your systems, and why cutting corners on cybersecurity is the most expensive decision a company can make.
- [How to Choose a Penetration Testing Company: A Practical Guide](https://kecman.co/blog/how-to-choose-a-penetration-testing-company.html): A practical guide to choosing the right penetration testing company. What to look for in experience and certifications, what a quality report should include even when clean, and how to avoid paying for an empty deliverable.
- [The Internet Never Forgets: How Archived Data Becomes a Security Risk](https://kecman.co/blog/the-internet-never-forgets.html): How the Wayback Machine, waybackurls, and web archives expose forgotten endpoints, leaked tokens, and UUID-based resources with broken access control.
- [My Hack The Box CWEE Review and Experience](https://kecman.co/blog/htb-cwee-review-experience.html): A detailed review of the Hack The Box Certified Web Exploitation Expert (CWEE) exam. Covers the 10-day exam format, study resources (HTB Academy Senior Web Penetration Tester path, PortSwigger Web Academy, PentesterLab), mindset advice on avoiding tunnel vision, the importance of debugging during exploitation, and why the exam is recommended for anyone specializing in web penetration testing or AppSec.
- [Bypassing Nginx ACLs in Python Applications](https://kecman.co/blog/bypassing-nginx-acls-python.html): How a trailing tab character can bypass Nginx regex ACLs when Python's .strip() normalizes the URL after the security check.
- [How One Google Search Led to a Critical Vulnerability](https://kecman.co/blog/google-search-critical-vulnerability.html): A critical vulnerability found in seconds using OSINT and Google dorking, exposing an API key with no usage limits.
